body.title: Bootstrap/seed/provision inventory — global-init vs device-local (T-inventory-classify-scattered-bootstraps)
body.method: grep -rilE 'bootstrap|seed|provision' --include=*.py (minus tests/.venv/pycache) yielded ~100 keyword hits; narrowed to real signal via CREATE TABLE/SCHEMA (shared-state DDL) + def bootstrap/seed/provision/enroll/ensure_tables. Key structural finding: the DOMINANT pattern is each DEVICE creating its OWN tables (per-device ensure_tables / CREATE TABLE) — correct blast-radius containment; those STAY device-local. Global-init is the small cross-instance set.
body.text:
# Bootstrap inventory — classified worklist (2026-06-28)

Discovery only (D-installer-absorbs-init). Each GLOBAL-INIT item becomes its own future move ticket; DEVICE-LOCAL stays put (moving it breaks containment).

## GLOBAL-INIT (cross-instance shared state — candidates to consolidate under the installer device)
- `unseen_university/global_kb.py` — `def bootstrap()` clones/pulls a KB repo, creates `global_kb.patterns` (Postgres), upserts patterns/*.jsonl. Cross-instance shared KB. **Already ticketed: T-home-global-kb-under-installer.**
- `scripts/search_index_bootstrap.py` — sole DDL creating `adc.search_index`, read by the Librarian search (`librarian/tools/search_tool.py`) AND written by `scraps/jobs/folder_indexer.py`. Cross-DEVICE shared search table. → future move ticket (decide installer-owned vs a search-subsystem owner; kept live for now, NOT dead).
- `unseen_university/migrations/m_*.py` (m_library, m_vault, m_devlab, m_devlab_intent, m_hubert_audit_flags) — idempotent shared-schema creation. ADJUDICATION: these are global schema-init, BUT they are organized as their own `migrations/` subsystem. Recommendation: keep `migrations/` as a distinct subsystem (a migration runner is not the same concern as instance provisioning); the installer may CALL the migration runner at provision time rather than absorbing the migration files. Flag for Akien at design time — do NOT fold blindly into installer.

## DEVICE-LOCAL (a device initializing its OWN state — STAYS; moving breaks blast-radius containment)
Each of these creates/owns a single device's own tables or registry:
- Per-device DDL / ensure_tables: intent/store.py, build_digester/digest_store.py, bus/pg_bus.py, evaluator/device.py, auditor/device.py, archivist/learning_pipeline.py, inference/capability_graph.py, postgres/shim.py, action_log.py, librarian/{palace_writer,tools/curation_tools,tools/proposal_tools}.py
- igor's own: igor/cognition/{blob_store,dreaming,focus_state,forensic_logger,proposals,shadow_reasoner,watch_problems}.py, igor/memory/{cortex,pending_replies}.py, igor/tools/{machine_manager,notebook,resource_manager}.py
- igor/acquisition/bootstrap.py — `bootstrap_channels()` registers igor's acquisition channels at import. Device-local.
- igor/tools/seed_*.py (seed_coding_standards, seed_strategic_goals, seed_tool_discovery, seed_persistent_relationships, seed_boredom_foreman, seed_facia_query, seed_inhibit_mid_conv_greeting, seed_reply_obligation_look, seed_cc_direction_habit, inhibition_seeder) — seed igor's OWN habits/patterns into its memory. Device-local.
- vault/seed.py — one-shot seeder of the vault device's own credential rows. Device-local (the vault is the owner of credentials).
- evaluator/uu_enrollment.py — evaluator's self-enrollment observe→learn loop. Device-local.

## DEAD (already purged 2026-06-28, T-purge-dead-bootstrap-scripts)
- scripts/palace_bootstrap.py, palace_cli.py, palace_seed_decisions.py, unified_node_adc_migration.py (retired Postgres-palace; git history preserves).

## NOT-INIT (keyword false matches — 'seed'/'bootstrap' in non-init senses; NO action)
- devlab/claudecode/seed_layer3_*.py + seed_layer4_*.py — graph-tree REASONING seed nodes (Hubert/DS inference layers), not instance-init.
- igor/cognition/* mentioning 'seed' as seed-of-thought / pattern activation (turn_pipeline, narrative_engine, wandering_search, etc.).
- devlab/claudecode/{audit_*,proof_emitter,memory_emit,session_manager,slate_manager,book_learner}.py — dev-process tooling; keyword incidental.

## ADJUDICATED (named in the ticket)
- `devlab/claudecode/bootstrap_learning.py` — bootstraps the inference KB graph from historical CC chat logs (idempotent, ON CONFLICT). It is CC DEV-TOOLING (lives in devlab/claudecode), not a device. It seeds a cross-instance knowledge graph, so it is global-KB-ADJACENT, but it is NOT an installer concern — it stays in devlab/claudecode as a CC tool. (If the inference KB is ever unified with global_kb, revisit.)
